claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 2^n - 2 is divisible by n.
predicate: n <= 1 or (is_prime(n) and (pow(2, n, n) == 2))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 3^n - 3 is divisible by n.
predicate: n <= 1 or (is_prime(n) and (pow(3, n, n) == 3))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 5^n - 5 is divisible by n.
predicate: n <= 1 or (is_prime(n) and (pow(5, n, n) == 5))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 6^n - 6 is divisible by n.
predicate: n <= 1 or (is_prime(n) and (pow(6, n, n) == 6))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 10^n - 10 is divisible by n.
predicate: n <= 1 or (is_prime(n) and (pow(10, n, n) == 10))

claim: For all odd integers n > 1, if n is prime then 2^n - 2 is divisible by 2n.
predicate: n <= 1 or (n % 2 == 0) or (not is_prime(n)) or (pow(2, n, 2*n) == 2)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then (2^n - 2) * (3^n - 3) is divisible by n^2.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n, n*n) - 2) * (pow(3, n, n*n) - 3) % (n*n) == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 2^n - 2 is divisible by 6.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n) - 2) % 6 == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 2^n - 2 is divisible by 12.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n) - 2) % 12 == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 2^n - 2 is divisible by 18.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n) - 2) % 18 == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 2^n - 2 is divisible by 36.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n) - 2) % 36 == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 2^n - 2 is divisible by n and by phi(n).
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n) - 2) % n == 0 and (pow(2, n) - 2) % phi(n) == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then (2^n - 2) + (3^n - 3) is divisible by n.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n) - 2 + pow(3, n) - 3) % n == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then (3^n - 3) + (5^n - 5) is divisible by n.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(3, n) - 3 + pow(5, n) - 5) % n == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then (2^n - 2) + (3^n - 3) + (5^n - 5) is divisible by n.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n) - 2 + pow(3, n) - 3 + pow(5, n) - 5) % n == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 2^n - 2 is divisible by tau(n).
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n) - 2) % tau(n) == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 3^n - 3 is divisible by tau(n).
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(3, n) - 3) % tau(n) == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then (2^n - 2) * (3^n - 3) is divisible by tau(n)^2.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or (((pow(2, n) - 2) * (pow(3, n) - 3)) % (tau(n) * tau(n)) == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 2^n - 2 is divisible by sigma(n) - n.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n) - 2) % (sigma(n) - n) == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 3^n - 3 is divisible by sigma(n) - n.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(3, n) - 3) % (sigma(n) - n) == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then (2^n - 2) + (3^n - 3) is divisible by (sigma(n) - n)^2.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n) - 2 + pow(3, n) - 3) % ((sigma(n) - n) * (sigma(n) - n)) == 0)

claim: For all odd integers n > 1, if n is prime then 2^n - 2 is divisible by n and by 2^(n-1) - 1.
predicate: n <= 1 or (n % 2 == 0) or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n) - 2) % n == 0 and (pow(2, n) - 2) % (pow(2, n-1) - 1) == 0)

claim: For all odd integers n > 1, if n is prime then 2^n - 2 is divisible by n * (2^(n-1) - 1).
predicate: n <= 1 or (n % 2 == 0) or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n) - 2) % (n * (pow(2, n-1) - 1)) == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then gcd(2^n - 2, n) = n.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or (gcd(pow(2, n) - 2, n) == n)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then gcd(3^n - 3, n) = n.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or (gcd(pow(3, n) - 3, n) == n)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then gcd((2^n - 2), (3^n - 3)) is divisible by n.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or ((gcd(pow(2, n) - 2, pow(3, n) - 3)) % n == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 2^n - 2 is divisible by 2 * phi(n).
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n) - 2) % (2 * phi(n)) == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 3^n - 3 is divisible by 3 * phi(n).
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(3, n) - 3) % (3 * phi(n)) == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then (2^n - 2) * (3^n - 3) is divisible by 6 * phi(n)^2.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or (((pow(2, n) - 2) * (pow(3, n) - 3)) % (6 * phi(n) * phi(n)) == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 2^n - 2 is divisible by the product of all primes less than n.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or (all((pow(2, n) - 2) % p == 0 for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p)))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 3^n - 3 is divisible by the product of all primes less than n.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or (all((pow(3, n) - 3) % p == 0 for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p)))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then (2^n - 2) * (3^n - 3) is divisible by the square of the product of all primes less than n.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or (all(((pow(2, n) - 2) * (pow(3, n) - 3)) % (p * p) == 0 for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p)))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 2^n - 2 is divisible by n and by (n-1)!.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n) - 2) % n == 0 and (pow(2, n) - 2) % factorial(n-1) == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 3^n - 3 is divisible by (n-1)!.
predicate: n <= 1 or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(3, n) - 3) % factorial(n-1) == 0)
